title: Cochlearia abulensis
taxonomy: Cochlearia abulensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is a member of the genus Cochlearia, which is commonly known as the scurvy-grass or sea rocket. The family Brassicaceae is a large and diverse group of plants that includes many species of cabbages, mustards, and wildflowers. The genus Cochlearia contains around 20 species of flowering plants, most of which are found in coastal areas of Europe, Asia, and North Africa. Cochlearia abulensis is thought to be endemic to the Azores, a group of islands in the Atlantic Ocean.

intro: Cochlearia abulensis, also known as Moroccan rockcress, is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cochlearia abulensis include isothiocyanates, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cochlearia abulensis is often prepared as a tea or tincture to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and sore throats, as well as to support digestion and reduce inflammation.
